title: The Best Books to Read This Summer, According to Isaac Fitzgerald and Yasmine Cheyenne
summary: Here are six beach reads to read in 2026 according to Isaac Fitzgerald and Yasmine Cheyenne.
story: Is it summer, or is it summer reading season? Both answers are correct. If you’re not sure where to start on the books front, Isaac Fitzgerald and Yasmine Cheyenne stopped by TODAY on June 29 to share a few of the books they’re loving this season.

Yasmine Cheyenne, who is also a wellness coach and a meditation expert, is the author of “The Comeback Era,” provided a few books that will transport you to a less stressful place (hopefully). One is the latest offering from a Read With Jenna author Jessica George.
"The Comeback Era" by Yasmine Cheyenne
Welcome to your comeback era.
Isaac Fitzgerald, meanwhile, is the author of memoirs and most recently the travelogue “American Rambler: Walking the Trail of Johnny Appleseed.”
"American Rambler: Walking the Trail of Johnny Appleseed" by Isaac Fitzgerald
A heartwarming novel that tells Fitzgerald's story about following the steps of Johnny Appleseed.
Below, check out their picks.
Isaac’s Picks
"Phoebe Berman's Gonna Lose It" by Brooke Averick
A funny, warm story about a hopeless romantic determined to lose her virginity before her 30th birthday.

What Isaac said: “Phoebe Berman’s Gonna Lose It’ is oh so warm and deeply charming — a novel about romance, friendship, anxiety, and the strange little deadlines we invent for becoming ourselves. In this case, well, losing one’s virginity before turning 30. Laugh-out-loud funny while still being tender and big-hearted, here is a love story in the truest sense, about learning to want honestly, messily, and entirely on your own terms.”
"Main Characters: A Novel" by Bobby Palmer
A love story of a pair whose paths unexpectedly cross throughout two decades, told from outside perspectives.

What Isaac said: “Main Characters’ is a brilliant book — a captivating love story told from the margins by the people who perhaps understand us before we understand ourselves. The book follows Clara and Seb across two decades as their lives brush up against one another’s, but is told from the perspective of everyone around them. A clever concept that totally works, here is a great summer read for anyone who has ever believed in the life-changing power of truly being seen.”
"This Vast Enterprise: A New History of Lewis & Clark" by Craig Fehrman
After years of research, this is an entertaining history of US history's two famous explorers.

What Isaac said: “An incredible feat, Craig Fehrman’s ‘This Vast Enterprise’ is a wildly entertaining history of Lewis and Clark — the product of more than half a decade of rigorous and immensely impressive research — that never loses sight of the adventure at its center. Fehrman brings this legendary expedition thrillingly to life by turning our attention beyond Lewis and Clark themselves, revealing the oft-overlooked people and perspectives that made the journey possible. Expansive, compelling, and alive on every page, this is a perfect summer read for America’s 250th anniversary.”
Yasmine’s Picks
"The Art of Loving You" by Natasha Bishop
The sequel to Bishop's "Only for the Week" of an intimate, spicy romance set during a bucket list road trip.

What Yasmine said: “Impossible to put down, this romance winds you through love, loss and the joy thats possible on the other side of change. If you love a plot that moves you as much as it inspires you, this is your pick!’
"Love by the Book: A Novel" by Jessica George
An honest story about the complications of adult friendships and new connections.

What Yasmine said: “A page-turner that puts words to what so many of us quietly feel about adult friendships — the growing pains, the unexpected shifts, and the beauty of who finds you in the in-between.”
"Waiting on a Friend: A Novel" by Natalie Adler
Natalie Adler's debut novel.

What Yasmine said: “A debut that somehow makes you feel more alive the deeper it pulls you into loss — an absolute love letter to a moment in history that still echoes today, with all the heart and humor to prove it.”

title: Former ‘Love Island USA’ Star Alannah Keyser Draws Backlash For Apology Video After Dismissal From Show
summary: “Love Island USA” fans criticize former contestant Alannah Keyser’s newest apology for her previous use of a racial slur.
story: “Love Island USA” fans don’t seem to be buying former contestant Alannah Keyser’s newest apology for her previous use of a racial slur, which ultimately led to her dismissal from the show.

In a TikTok video on June 27, Keyser addressed a resurfaced clip, in which she was seen using a racial slur while singing the lyrics to the 2019 Roddy Rich song “The Box.” Keyser told viewers that the video was “from six years ago, and that word is just not in my vocabulary anymore.”
“I’m sorry to whoever saw that video and has been offended by it,” Keyser said in the TikTok video. “That was never my intention.”
Keyser, a 21-year-old film student from Miami, also said that she doesn’t support “racism or discrimination of any kind, and I never have.”
“This has definitely been a learning lesson for me, and it sucks that I didn’t get a chance to really show my personality and who I am,” she said.
Peacock did not have further comment on the issue.
The video has received thousands of comments from viewers, many of whom said they found her apology insufficient.
“Genuinely the worst apology I’ve ever seen,” said one TikTok user, who received more than 12,000 likes for the comment.

Keyser, who joined the show as a Casa Amor bombshell during the June 21 episode of the steamy series, stopped appearing on the program after the June 25 episode, Peacock confirmed to TODAY.com. The new video is her first that addresses the controversy. Several fans criticized her TikTok apology, saying that the racial slur was never acceptable to use, and that she should have known better six years ago.
“To be saying that in 2020 is actually even more wild,” said another TikTok user.

Others took issue with the way in which she appeared to avoid kissing cast members KC Chandler and Sincere Rhea, who are both Black, during the show’s heart rate challenge. Keyser did not address her behavior in the challenge during the apology video, but wrote in the caption that her “chats/kisses with the other boys were unfortunately not aired.”
“this falls completely flat when we literally saw her discriminate against the black men on the cast. we have eyes girl,” one X user said.
Another X user wrote that “It’s not just the fact you said the word, it’s also how you visibly treated the black male contestants differently.”

Keyser is the latest in a string of “Love Island USA” cast members who were removed from the show after major controversies. Vasana Montgomery, for example, was featured in an introduction video for Season 8, only to be dismissed from the cast the next day after clips of her using a racial slur resurfaced. And in Season 7, cast members Yulissa Escobar and Cierra Ortega were both separately removed after their previous use of racial slurs came to light.

title: Josh Gad Honors ‘Comedy Idol’ Mel Brooks’ 100th Birthday With Emotional Instagram Post
summary: Josh Gad shared an Instagram post featuring his “comedy Idol” Mel Brooks for the legend’s 100th birthday on Sunday
story: Mel Brooks is entering his centennial year with some kind words of admiration from a fellow comedian.

Actor and singer Josh Gad shared some sweet photos on social media with Brooks in honor of the comedy legend’s big 100th birthday on June 28. Gad, who co-wrote the upcoming sequel to Brooks’ iconic 1987 “Star Wars” parody film “Spaceballs,” had some lovely things to say about the centenarian.

“For the past five years, I have had the greatest gift in my life… collaborating with and working alongside my comedy idol,” Gad wrote on Instagram of Brooks, who is also reprising his role as the beloved character Yogurt in the “Spaceballs” sequel. “Today we celebrate 100 years of his life and legacy. He has made us laugh and hurt on the big screen, the small screen, the stage and even on our record players.”
Gad added: “There is nothing he hasn’t won, nothing he hasn’t achieved, nothing he hasn’t proven and yet at 100, he continues to surprise and thrill us.”

The series of 20 photos posted to Gad’s social media features snapshots from his time working with Brooks on the “Spaceballs” sequel. In nearly every shot, Brooks, known for his joyful disposition, is grinning from ear-to-ear.

Citing a number of Brooks’ ongoing projects including the West End production of the legend’s iconic musical ‘The Producers’ and the upcoming television adaptation of 1974 hit “Young Frankenstein,” Gad wrote that the ‘best is still yet to come’ for the 100-year-old.
“I love you Mel. I cannot wait for the world to see this next chapter,” Gad wrote. “Thank you for your trust and your partnership.”
Brooks, who was born in 1926 in Brooklyn, is critically acclaimed for his work at the helm of a number of boundary-pushing films. He previously credited his longevity to his sense of humor.
“It’s an amazing sound, people laughing at something I created,” Brooks told People in January. “Making comedy is a great job. It keeps you sane and happy. It gives you a reason to be alive.”

title: ‘Pineapple Street’ Author Jenny Jackson Shares the Opening Lines of Her Next Book — and the Real Incident that Inspired Them
summary: Jenny Jackson shares the real life incident behind the opening line of her second book “The Shampoo Effect.”
story: It is a truth universally acknowledged that a good story starts with a good first line. In TODAY’s Cold Open, authors tease their buzzy upcoming release by revealing and explaining its first line, paragraph or page.
“Pineapple Street” author Jenny Jackson always knew how she was going to start her second book, “The Shampoo Effect.”
“That was always going to be the opening line,” she tells TODAY.com.
The book’s first words were borrowed directly from an incident in her sister-in-law’s life that she can’t stop laughing about.

In the first scene, Caroline Lash, a New Yorker who takes a writing residency in Massachusetts, steps on a doughnut and gets some help with clean-up from a stranger, whom she later learns is named Van. It’s an instant meet-cute.

It could have been a Boston Cream, puffy white, the inside sweet vanilla pudding, but instead it was a jelly donut, glazed with sugar, filled with the sort of fluorescent red goo that could only be created in a food science lab. Caroline didn't even realize she'd stepped on the thing.
She says something similar happened to her sister-in-law: “She is the nicest human in the world, and the stuff that happens to her, you wouldn’t believe.”
Once, she was running late to a job interview in Boston and “stomped” on a jelly doughnut, per Jackson’s retelling.
“She goes to the lobby of the job interview and realizes there is red jelly all over her pants,” she says.
She had to make a quick outfit change using a pashmina she happened to have in her bag.
“She takes off her pants, shoves them in a bag, wraps a pashmina around her waist like a skirt, and does the whole job interview wearing like a hippie pashmina skirt,” Jackson says through laughter.

“Every time I think about it, I get the giggles. I have been unable to stop laughing about this for so long. So when I thought about Boston jelly doughnuts, it had to be the opening scene for the book,” she continues.
Jackson says her sister-in-law doesn’t know about the scene yet. “I’m so excited to give it to her. She’s going to freak out.”

It was the rest of the book that was a challenge. “The Shampoo Effect,” chosen as Read With Jenna’s July 2026 pick, is a departure from the rarefied Brooklyn world that her bestselling debut “Pineapple Street” was set.

Jackson, who is also the editorial director of fiction at Knopf, says she “couldn’t get comfortable” with the idea that people associated her with the characters from “Pineapple Street,” which features an affluent Brooklyn family.
“I’m not a one percenter, and I didn’t grow up in New York. Part of writing ‘The Shampoo Effect’ for me was subconsciously a corrective to write about my hometown and where I’m from,” she says.
She wanted to write a book for people who already liked “Pineapple Street” and people who didn’t, which she calls a “terrible way” to go into a novel.
The book is set in a similar coastal Massachusetts town to where she grew up, Ipswich, and follows a childhood friend group now in their 30s.
She calls the book “a story of love and marriage, and that moment in your early 30s where you have your real coming of age.”
To create the friend group, she “borrowed bits and pieces from a zillion sort of characters” that she grew up with — beyond just her sister-in-law.
For example, she recalls a childhood friend whose father was a beer distributor and lived in a fancy house — which makes it into the book through the character of Bailey.
Her husband is the inspiration for Van. “He read the book, and he was lying in bed. He was like, ‘Really, honey?’ I’m like, ‘Sorry,’” she says.
The heart of the book is inspired by the “fascinating thing that happens” when your old friends start to take different life paths.

“One person settles down first, one person leans in professionally first, one person has a baby first. It’s easy to that as a judgment on yourself and your own decisions,” she says. “You innately want your friends to be on your same page. It keeps you in conversation with each other, and also sort of cosigns your life choices.”

title: New Details Emerge About Prince Harry and Meghan Markle’s UK Visit in July
summary: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle will be traveling to the United Kingdom in July. Their children will also join them.
story: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle will soon travel to the United Kingdom for a special trip that will include appearances at multiple public events.

The couple, who stepped away from their duties as senior members of the British royal family in 2021, will fly across the pond next month with 7-year-old Prince Archie and 5-year-old Princess Lilibet, a source confirmed to NBC News.

Prince Harry and Markle, officially the Duchess of Sussex, have been raising their children in Montecito, California, for the past few years. The family will return to his homeland and from July 7 to July 11 will celebrate one year until the Invictus Games are held in Birmingham, England. British media first reported news of the U.K. visit.

In addition to promoting the July 2027 games, which are a multi-day sporting competition, Prince Harry is also set to make appearances at charity events for WellChild and Scotty’s Little Soldiers, the source tells NBC News. Prince Archie and Princess Lilibet will not be in attendance at any of the public events and the couple does not plan to have media interviews.

Buckingham Palace did not confirm to NBC News if Prince Harry and Markle will reunite with King Charles III during their stay. But, the source told NBC News that the Sussexes were offered housing at a royal residence for the duration of their visit.
The family will stay for some of their time in the U.K. in a royal residence, the source confirmed to NBC News.

As for the family’s security protection while abroad, a government spokesperson told NBC News, “It is our long-standing policy not to provide detailed information on those arrangements, as doing so could compromise their integrity and affect individuals’ security.”

Last year, Prince Harry reunited with his father for the first time in 19 months when he flew home in September 2025. The father and son spent time at the king’s residence, Clarence House, in London and had a private tea, Buckingham Palace officials confirmed at the time.

Prince Harry was in London to reconnect with some charities and organizations he partners with. The solo visit came after he shared in a May 2025 interview with the BBC that he wanted to mend the fractured relationship with his family.

“I would love reconciliation with my family. There’s no point in continuing to fight anymore. Life is precious. I don’t know how much longer my father has. It would be nice to reconcile,” he said at the time.

Prince Harry was interviewed after he lost an appeal to get his British security detail restored. He said the decision was a “surprise” and he was “devastated,” noting that he needed a higher level of security to bring his wife and children to the U.K.
“I can’t see a world in which I would be bringing my wife and children back to the U.K. at this point. And the things that they’re going to miss are, well, everything,” he shared.

He continued, “I love my country, I always have done, despite what some people in that country have done ... and I think that it’s really quite sad that I won’t be able to show my children my homeland.”
Markle showed a glimpse into their life as a family of four in the states recently when she posted a rare photo on Instagram of her husband and children to celebrate Father’s Day.
Below the snap, which showed Prince Archie and Princess Lilibet embracing their father, she wrote, “They’re so lucky to have you. We all are.”

title: USWNT Star Jaedyn Shaw Shares Her World Cup Memories and Career Moment She’d Like to Relive
summary: Jaedyn Shaw shares her World Cup memories, as well as what she’s most looking forward to as the tournament is underway and more.
story: Jaedyn Shaw remembers being a kid, not knowing if her dreams of being a soccer star would pan out, “but having the belief” that it could happen.
The 21-year-old United States Women’s National Team (USWNT) star tells TODAY.com that when training as a young girl, she understood the sacrifices it took “to achieve my dream.”

At 17, Shaw signed with the National Women’s Soccer League, kick-starting her professional career. Now, with a FIFA U-20 Women’s World Cup and an Olympic gold medal under her belt, she’s looking forward to watching the United States Men’s National Team score big wins during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where she’ll be a spectator of the international tournament for the first time.

“To see a World Cup on our home soil is something that’s also really special. It’s so cool to see all of the players that we’ve seen growing up on TV, being in the most random cities in the country,” the Adidas athlete says.
Below, in her own words, Shaw shares her World Cup memories, as well as what she’s most looking forward to with the tournament underway.
I’m going to have to go with the 2015 Women’s World Cup. Carli Lloyd getting a hat trick from half field. That’s probably my biggest World Cup memory.

I was with the U.S. National Team, playing in my home city, Frisco, in the FC Dallas Stadium, where I grew up going to games and training. I play for FC Gotham right now. We ended up going down a goal and we ended up coming back, and I scored the winning goal in my home city, home state, basically in front of all of my friends and family that came. That moment is probably a moment that I’ll remember forever.

It’s going be really exciting to watch the U.S. Men’s National Team play. It’ll be really cool to see how they come out mentally. I think they have the ability to go very far in this tournament, and of course I want them to do well. Also, seeing (Lionel) Messi playing in Alabama, they (were) staying in Kansas City in the hotels that we stay in as well. It’s really crazy to see that they’re training on the same pitches, playing the same stadiums that some of us will play on, or have played in. It’s really cool to have the opportunity to watch.

title: ‘Love Island USA’s’ Sol Dean Reveals the Couple She’s ‘Glad to Be Wrong’ About
summary: “Love Island USA” Season 8 bombshell Sol Mýa Dean’s time in Fiji was short but tumultuous — entering the villa on Day 7 and being dumped on Day 15.
story: “Love Island USA” Season 8 bombshell Sol Mýa Dean’s time in Fiji was short but tumultuous — entering the villa on Day 7 and being dumped on Day 15.
The Southern California-native quickly formed a connection with Sincere Rhea upon entering the villa and coupled up with him on Day 10, per America’s vote.
Their connection was cut short just four episodes later, when Sincere chose to rekindle his relationship with Melanie Moreno during the “You’ve Got Mail” challenge.

The shake-up left Sol coupled up with fellow bombshell Caleb McDaniel by default, but the pair only spent one day together before being voted among the least popular couples. That night, Sol was selected by a group of male islanders to be sent home alongside Gabriel Vasconcelos, who entered the villa as a bombshell on Day 2.
Upon hearing the news of the vote, Sol told the islanders that no one seemed open to getting to know her when she arrived, and she thanked the cast for putting her out of her “misery.”
Rather than shedding tears on her way out the door, she paused a final time to tell the islanders to make sure future newcomers don’t feel “excluded” like she did in the villa.
Below, Sol shares more about what really went down — from the emotional pie challenge to Sincere’s speech at her dumping — and which islander she thinks should win it all.
This interview has been lightly edited for clarity.

I think it was a lot of stuff. I don’t know, it was just rough going in there, it was a lot. There was a lot that was just happening, and all I’m gonna say is that it was more than two. But it was just emotional, like hearing them pick me for answers that they didn’t really get to know me for. I feel like that was my big thing. I just came in, they knew my name is Sol, and I’m a bombshell, and nothing else, so I just kind of felt a little dissed where I’m like, where are these warranted when no one’s gotten to know me?

Maybe applying more pressure with the guys. But I also want things to feel mutual, and if they’re happy and content where they’re at, then I’m not going to overly want to pursue something. So it was hard, kind of finding the silver lining of what I should do and what I shouldn’t do, but I felt like (with) the experience I did the best with the cards I was dealt.
I don’t know if he wanted to get back for me humbling him, but I was just like, OK, this is like the finale cherry on the top of you being able to just kind of dump me.

I don’t know, I didn’t feel like the situation was fair, honestly, and him saying that I wasn’t in a progressive couple when he knows that I obviously put most of my time and energy into getting to know him, because what he was telling me versus what he was saying to other people was different. But me, I’m someone that likes to take things at face value, and I mean, I won’t really have doubt until there’s a reason, so I mean, I was feeling like a bunch of different ways.
At least, I don’t know, someone else could have said it.

I just feel like I bit my tongue a lot, honestly, to keep things cordial and to have peace for living with these people 24/7. It’s also being filmed, so you’re kind of trying to navigate things in the best way, and I don’t really know if they knew like the effect that they were doing to me in the villa. And I feel like that’s just more so something I want to touch on when they do come out, and how they made that not a wonderful experience for me … There’s no shade. I hope a lot of them are still in their couples, but do I want to explain how they were making me feel while my time in the villa was very short and present there? I do want to kind of address that with them. So, that’s mainly my focus for that.

The hate on Trinity is a super misconception. On “Aftersun,” I feel like a lot of people kind of misconstrued my words, but in the beginning I said I wanted to get to know Trinity more because she was hilarious. She’s funny as hell. I felt like after watching some of the episodes, too, it just kind of made me validate the feelings I was feeling in the villa about what was being said and why there was just like, random tension. But again, I feel like the misconception would be Trinity and what I said on “Aftersun.”

I only said what I said on “Aftersun” because Bryce did pull Kayda multiple times and get rejected. It’s just kind of like, in my mind, if I’m keeping it plain simple at face value of what I’m seeing, if you were happy with your couple, you wouldn’t want to keep exploring, so that was just kind of more my premise on that. I didn’t say I think that she’s prettier than her. I could have obviously delivered it better, and I feel like that’s something I need to work on ... There’s no hate on Trinity. I think she’s amazing, and honestly, out of everyone, I think she should win this, so I’ll stand on that.

I haven’t really watched Casa Amor, I only watched like the first episode, but like, the little clips I have been seeing, I just saw both of them kind of like crying, I believe for each other, and I’m glad I’m wrong.

When you’re in the villa, like, a day feels like a month, and I already know that they had a connection brewing, and I wasn’t saying that they didn’t have a connection brewing, it was more so, like, Bryce just kept pulling Kayda and got rejected, so it was like, I didn’t say that they didn’t have a connection. But I’m honestly glad, I’m glad to even see this pan out. I think it’s great. I’m glad to be wrong on that, I do feel like they have a connection.

I feel like it’ll make the girls just more open, if anything, and I feel like that was maybe the point. The girls are super locked in and just super forgiving with their guys, but if you got double the men, and you just saw how your current person was moving, like it’s, “Go do your big one, girl,” you know. Go find someone else in Casa and just give other people a fair shot. So I hope that they’re doing that, honestly.

I kind of knew the boys were gonna go crazy in Casa, just since they’re not being watched. So, I don’t know if I could boil it down to just one person. I couldn’t really answer that, but I feel like all of them kind of just felt like the chains were being broken loose. It was like a brand new show happening again, having a bunch of people get introduced, you get to know everyone without, like, a current partner watching, or like reprimanding you for it. I don’t know, I feel like they all were kind of, like, really excited from what I saw.

Watching it from a viewer’s perspective is interesting. It’s a little cringe, but it’s also interesting because I’m like, completely myself all the time, and I feel like, again, you guys get one hour out of 24. Watching it back is kind of just like, there’s a lot of things that just were happening that I wasn’t aware of, so maybe asking like questions more, I feel like, again, I take a lot of things at face value, so maybe kind of trying to get the scoop, and what’s going on. Why is blank upset, or whatever it is. It’s interesting watching yourself back.

title: Melanie Martinez Reveals Name of Next Album
summary: Melanie Martinez has revealed exclusively on TODAY the name of her upcoming album will be “Elysium.”
story: Melanie Martinez has already revealed the name of her next album, just three months after the release of “Hades.”
The former “Voice” contestant dropped the news exclusively on TODAY June 26, saying her 2026 album, “Hades” is Part 1 of a two-album effort.
“There’s another album that I haven’t said the name yet, but maybe I’ll say it now. Why not?” the “Dollhouse” singer said as the crowd on the plaza went wild.
“The second album is called ‘Elysium.’ That’s all I’ll say, but it’s a utopian reference, so it’s the other part. It’s the counterpart to it,” she added, as the crowd continued howling.
While she didn’t reveal too much information about the album itself, she did talk about how “Hades,” which topped the Billboard rock and alternative albums chart in April, came together.

“With ‘Hades,’ it was just kind of reflecting on the current times, and the world feels very dystopian, so I wanted to make a dystopian record,” she said, noting she was inspired by the book “Parable of the Sower” by Octavia E. Butler.
“I thought it was time to make an album about all of the topics that I felt were really kind of heavy on my heart and I’m just really happy that resonate with it,” she said.
Martinez has quite the loyal fanbase, which has grown since she appeared on “The Voice” in 2012. It’s a rise that has surprised her.
“I’m always in awe, and unbelievably grateful,” she said.

“And I just feel lucky that I get to do what I love and that I get to put on shows and create safe spaces for people all over the world to come together who are like-minded and creative and passionate,” she added. “And it’s inspiring and I just want to do it for the rest of my life, so I’m just really grateful.”

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Removes Another Contestant, Alannah Keyser, For Using Racial Slur
summary: “Love Island USA” contestant Alannah Keyser will no longer appear on the program after a video of her using a racial slur surfaced.
story: “Love Island USA” contestant Alannah Keyser, who joined the show on the June 21 episode of the hit Peacock series, will no longer appear on the program following the June 25 episode, the streaming service has confirmed to TODAY.com.
Her removal comes after a video resurfaced in which she used a racial slur while singing the lyrics to the 2019 Roddy Rich song “The Box.”
Keyser, 21, hails from Miami and had served as one of the Casa Armor bombshells. She appeared to have made a connection with Zach Georgiou.

“Love Island USA” has removed other cast members before. Vasana Montgomery was among the islanders who appeared in an introduction video for Season 8 on May 28. Clips of her using a racial slur surfaced shortly after that and she was dismissed from the cast the next day. She did not appear on the June 2 premiere.

A pair of Season 7 islanders were also removed from the villa in 2025. Yulissa Escobar appeared in one episode before viewers were informed that she was no longer on the show after clips of her using racial slurs on a podcast came to light.

“In those clips, I used a word I never should’ve used, a racial slur,” she later wrote, in part, on Instagram. “I used it ignorantly, not fully understanding the weight, history, or pain behind it. I wasn’t trying to be offensive or harmful, but I recognize now that intention doesn’t excuse impact. And the impact of that word is real. It’s tied to generations of trauma, and it is not mine to use.”
Cierra Ortega, who was also on Season 7, left the show toward the end of that season after videos of her using racial slurs surfaced.
She later apologized in a TikTok video.

“I completely agree with the network’s decision to remove me from the villa. I think that this is something that deserved punishment, and the punishment is absolutely been received, for sure,” she said.
Escobar and Ortega departed the show under similar circumstances, which Escobar noted.
“I’m not justifying what I did, I know what I did and what I said was wrong, and I know what Cierra said was wrong and I know it hurt communities,” she said about Ortega in a TikTok video.

title: ‘Love Island USA’s’ Gabriel Reacts to His Elimination, Reveals Where He Stands With Beatriz
summary: The girls of “Love Island USA” said they chose to dump Gabriel Vasconcelos from the villa because “he’s constantly looking for the next best thing, and we don’t feel like the villa will ever bring him someone he’s content with.”Gabriel says he’s still asking why
story: The girls of “Love Island USA” said they chose to dump Gabriel Vasconcelos from the villa because “he’s constantly looking for the next best thing, and we don’t feel like the villa will ever bring him someone he’s content with.”
Gabriel says he’s still asking why.

The bombshell entered the villa on Day 2 and quickly formed connections with multiple islanders. He first coupled up with Beatriz Hatz before viewers voted to pair him with bombshell Jen Terry, a connection Gabriel says he was still growing when his journey came to an end.

After viewers voted again, this time for their favorite couple, Gabriel and Jen were left vulnerable. The boys safe from the vote chose to send Sol Mýa Dean home, arguing she was the only girl at risk of elimination not in a strong couple, sparing Jen.
But instead of Sol’s unattached counterpart, Caleb McDaniel, the girls chose to send Gabriel home, claiming he hadn’t fully committed to one relationship and would likely not settle.

In a conversation with TODAY.com, Gabriel opens up about his reaction to the reasoning behind his elimination, the criticism he received for exploring multiple connections and the moments viewers didn’t get to see during his time in Fiji.
This interview has been lightly edited for clarity.

I’m still asking myself, “Why?” Is it because I was exploring? Is it because I was doing the “Love Island” experience? I was the “craziest one,” you know? I don’t see any point about that answer. I’m sorry, but I’m completely honest and upfront with my answer, so everyone knows that ... I just had to accept that, the fact that I was leaving.

I mean, he’s right. He’s right. We have different backgrounds, like, it doesn’t mean that I’m ... lustful, you know. Like Brazilians, we are different, and I was literally having a good time with the girls that I was with, nobody was complaining about it.

Not even Jen was understanding the fact that I was leaving. She was like, oh, s---, like, I don’t believe that. Because it wasn’t making sense. But I’m so glad that the guys were defending me because they knew about how I am, how I was treating the girls.

In this case, like, Jen ... they know that my connection with Jen was growing. And yeah, like me and her know about it. That’s why I’m not worried about anything else, and I know that what I did and that was right.

All that I have to say about it, like, all the Latin language, they’re more romantic. So when I’m gonna talk about something specific, especially for feelings, I can be more — I can give more details of how I’m feeling. Not that I’m gonna express perfect in Portuguese, but just differently, you know? And I can give more. I can go over more about it, and that’s how I feel.

I feel like English, I also can do it, but it’s not the same way. It’s not the same vibe. The thing is, if I hear, “I love you” from someone, I know that people are gonna be loving me, but I feel if I hear “eu te amo,” which means “love you” in Portuguese or in Spanish, it means more for me.

But that’s one thing that it’s hard to explain for everyone because you just feel it. Like, if I hear “eu te amo” from my mom, it’s different than if I hear “love you” — I’m gonna think that she’s lying to me, because, you know, that’s just something different, it’s hard to explain.

I think it was the fact that most of my conversations with the guys and with the girls were not shown. You know, it’s literally 24 hours in one hour, a lot of people talking, and if they decided they just had to put a few moments of me like, making out, that was it.

I don’t know, I mean, I think I was flirting more than everyone else, and they only got those moments. People saw me talking with Sol (for) like 5 seconds, then we went to the bed together, then I started kissing her. No, no, no! We had a conversation at least for 30 minutes before. We were talking in front of the pool for, I don’t know, like 15 minutes and then we went upstairs. We started talking like in the chairs in front of each other for 15 minutes and then we went to the bed.
So like, nobody’s seen that, and it was looking like, oh, I went upstairs with her, I say, oh, I know what I want, and “Boom.” No, but it wasn’t that, and that’s funny.
We were watching it together, like, “Oh s---, that’s crazy.”

I think both of my connections, they are not showing most of our things. Because we were talking the whole day, not specific when we’re going to chat. When I was pulling them for a chat, OK, I was pulling them, but during the day, we were talking about a lot of things. I feel like we lost at least 90% of conversation.

Because if I was being just crazy, just making out, probably they wouldn’t be crying for me. People need to think about it, like, “Why they were crying for Gabriel?” or, “Why were they feeling sad?” or something. Because they had something else, they had some different feelings, because we were having a good time literally. But that’s one thing that I just, I was like, as we were leaving the villa, we know that. That’s life.
Yeah, we talked. We talked two days ago. Yeah, we are good.
No. No, I don’t have any regrets about anything.

I  really appreciate everyone who is changing their opinion about me because they’re already noticing the way that I am. When I had the chance to talk on “Aftersun,” they were like, “Oh s---, like he knows how to express himself,” you know? And I like that because I am like that. I was one of the guys who was most like making jokes with everyone and talking s---. I was always like present with everyone.

That’s why I was like, “Oh, s---, I’m not — where’s myself in this episode?” But I’m good with myself, that’s the most important thing. I know what I’ve done, and in the villa, everyone knows how I was treating them, and yeah, I was literally being myself and that’s the most important for me.
Mm, good question. Everyone was asking me the same thing. Oh, guys. “Oh, Gabriel, we are missing you in Casa Amor.” This and that. But, guys, I’m not there. I’m at my casa!

title: ‘X-Men’ Star Tyler Mane Opens Up About Breast Cancer Diagnosis: ‘Check Yourselves, Men’
summary: Tyler Mane reveals how he was diagnosed with male breast cancer. The “X-Men” actor and wrestler shares his symptom, treatment and risk factor for the disease.
story: Tyler Mane never imagined the hard lump that appeared on the left side of his chest would turn out to be breast cancer.

The “X-Men” actor and former wrestler says he first noticed it last fall, but wasn’t overly concerned. He thought it would go away. It was his wife, Renae, who urged him to get the lump checked out and removed this spring.
The diagnosis: invasive ductal carcinoma.
It was caught early, at Stage 1, when it hadn’t yet spread. Genetic testing revealed Mane has a gene mutation that increases his risk of breast cancer and other types, too.
“It never even crossed my mind that I would be having male breast cancer,” Mane, 61, who lives in Atlanta, tells TODAY.com in a digital exclusive interview.
“I was being conscious of heart conditions, but never breast cancer… I really want to say, ‘Check yourselves, men.’”
He announced his diagnosis on Instagram this month, calling it “super rare.”
Fewer than 1% of breast cancer cases occur in men, according to the American Cancer Society. Mane is one of 2,670 men expected to be diagnosed with the disease in the U.S. in 2026.
For men, the average lifetime risk of getting breast cancer is about 1 in 755, compared to 1 in 8 for women.
Mane going public “is a valuable reminder that men can get breast cancer,” Dr. Harold Burstein, director of breast cancer education at the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, told NBC News.
For many men, “it’s just not widely known that it’s a possibility,” Burstein added.
How Mane Discovered the Breast Cancer
Mane says his first reaction was to keep his diagnosis secret, calling it “kind of embarrassing.”
He explains he’s always been self-conscious about his chest.
“It’s been an issue for me because that’s where I carry my fat,” Mane says.
“All the way through high school, I had a fattier chest, meatier chest, and some kids can be cruel and make fun of you.”

When the former wrestler started a health journey about two years ago, he had a DEXA scan to measure his body fat percentage and was shocked to discover it came in at 33%. Mane then started working out to get back into shape, and it dropped to 16% last fall. That’s when he began noticing the lump in his left breast, close to his nipple.
“That was pretty much the only sign that I had,” he says.
“(But) as a guy, you just put stuff off. You go, ‘I’ll get better, I’ll be fine. Don’t worry about it, it’ll go away.’”
In the spring, Mane’s brother died of a massive heart attack. Health was on the family’s mind, and Mane’s wife insisted he have a doctor look at the lump.
His primary care physician “kind of dismissed it,” but said he could go to a dermatologist, Mane recalls. The dermatologist sent him to a surgeon, who removed the lump on April 1.
The biopsy results revealed it was invasive ductal carcinoma, the most common type of breast cancer in men, according to the National Library of Medicine.
“That’s when my life changed,” Mane says.
Genetic Testing Reveals Risk Factor
He underwent a PET scan and had three lymph nodes removed from his left armpit. All came back clear, confirming it was Stage 1 cancer. No more breast tissue needed to be removed.

Genetic testing revealed that Mane has the BRCA2 mutation, which increases a person’s risk of developing breast cancer and other cancers. “I am definitely a mutant,” he jokes, adding that it’s important information for his two children.

Other testing showed Mane’s removed tumor was fast-growing, so he started four rounds of chemotherapy to make sure no cancer cells would be left behind. His first session was on June 8, and he has three weeks in between the treatments.
The former wrestler does squats and pushups to keep up his strength.

“Right now, I’m feeling fine, but it has been a roller coaster,” he says, noting his facial hair is starting to fall out. “I was very tired, resting all the time, but you have to keep a strong mindset to come through this.”
Mane takes an estrogen blocker since his cancer was hormone receptor (HR) positive, which means it grows in response to estrogen.
He’ll undergo radiation once the chemotherapy is finished.
Mission to Raise Awareness

Mane is grateful to all the fans who’ve reached out to support and encourage him. His next project is “Mane AF,” a podcast he plans to do with his wife that focuses on “dealing with midlife mayhem and marriage,” he says. It’s launching in about a month.

But his big mission is to raise awareness about breast cancer in men. Men have reached out to him on social media saying they wish they had gotten checked earlier, before their disease reached Stage 3 or 4.

Men are more likely than women to die after being diagnosed with breast cancer, studies have found. One reason is that more men are diagnosed once the disease has already spread, which is likely due to a lack of awareness about breast cancer in men and screening for it, researchers noted.
Mane wants other men to know they’re not alone. He’s no longer embarrassed about his diagnosis.
“Becoming an advocate for it is something that I felt that I had to do,” he says.
“There’s no use hiding from stuff like that, because our bodies are our bodies, and we’re designed the way we are, and we have one go ‘round in life and make the best of it.”

title: ‘Voicemails For Isabelle’ Is a Netflix Hit. Here’s What Critics Are Saying
summary: What critics are saying about the new from-com “Voicemails for Isabelle” which is an audience hit.
story: “Voicemails for Isabelle,” which dropped on Netflix in June, is a rom-com with depth. The movie earns some real laughs, all while hitting some deep commentary on grief, sisterhood and the barriers within digital connection.
The film follows a pastry chef, Jill (Zoey Deutch), who, after the loss of her sister, Isabelle (Ciara Bravo), turns to her voicemail box to process her emotions.
Small problem: Isabelle’s number has been reassigned to someone else. Enter Wes (Nick Robinson), a real estate agent who is on the market to learn more about the mysterious voice spamming his phone.
With a 91% Rotten Tomatoes audience score, viewers seem to be walking away from “Voicemails for Isabelle” feeling very satisfied by this Deutch and Robinson team-up.
But what are the critics saying? Read below to find out.
The New York Times

With her lead role alongside Glen Powell from the 2018 film “Set It Up,” Deutch is no stranger to the rom-com genre. Writing for the New York Times, Glenn Kenney highlights her performance as a standout.
“The appealing Zoey Deutch is the best reason to watch “Voicemails for Isabelle,” he writes.

As Wes’ interest in learning more about Jill builds up, he flies from Texas to California to not-so sneakily run into her. Kenney draws comparisons between Wes’ efforts and rom-com inspirations the writers possibly drew from.
“Wes does other deeds reminiscent of the 1999 article in The Onion: ‘Romantic-Comedy Behavior Gets Real-Life Man Arrested,’ he writes.
Kenney ultimately lands on the film being a bit too predicable.
He writes, “One watches the blossoming romance between Wes and Jill with, of course, a certain dread about the inevitable shoe dropping.”
Deadline
Chemistry saves the day in the review by Pete Hammond, chief film critic for Deadline.

Although he finds Netflix “sometimes getting it right, sometimes getting it wrong” with this film, and similar to Kenney, faults its predictability, Hammond says the movie ”rises above the familiar with the sheer delight of the chemistry between its two stars.”
Hammond identifies the film’s rollercoaster ride of emotions quite simply: “You’ll laugh. You’ll cry. All that stuff.”
“Voicemails for Isabelle” rises above for Hammond through “the couple at the center of it all.”

He especially praises Deutch’s performance, saying it “works on every level” and calling her “a knockout that qualifies her a spot in the rom-com hall of fame alongside Julia Roberts, Kate Hudson and Meg Ryan.”

But wait — Hammond didn’t forget about her male co-star. “Robinson also has great appeal, perfectly balancing a role that might come across as more cynical in other hands, but he nails it,” he writes.
“Together they are winning.”
The Wrap
The Wrap concludes that this popular new film is only “an enthusiastic but misguided romance that only sometimes lives up to its references,” according to William Bibbiani.
While he believes the film “doesn’t want us to think Wes is a creep,” Bibbiani has some strong words for Wes’ character or any fans of his.
“If Wes got what was coming to him every time he did something creepy he’d be an unrecognizable bruise in the shape of a human by the end of this movie,” he writes.
Even as the film progresses, this critic cannot escape his Wes hatred.

“To Robinson’s credit he almost makes the character work, although it takes 90% of the movie to get there,” he writes. “The conclusion, bolstered by a laugh out loud Taylor Swift joke, finds Wes defeated and humbled.”
Decider
Jesse Hassenger awards the female lead as “the rom-com queen of 2026” with this new film.
With “Voicemails For Isabelle,” this critic thinks Deutch has found her sweet spot: “characters who are chipper, even mischievous, with the heart of a classic romantic heroine.”
“She’s just synthetic enough to power through a Netflix rom-com with superhuman energy, and just sincere enough to make you care about it,” Hassenger continues.

title: Casey McQuiston Unveils Cover of ‘Red, White & Royal Blue’ Sequel Novella
summary: See the cover of Casey McQuiston’s “Red, White & Royal Blue: The Private Correspondence,” and find out what it’s about and how it’s related to the first book and movie.
story: Casey McQuiston will continue the story of the star-crossed romance between the first son of the United States and a prince of England with the novella, “Red, White & Royal Blue: The Private Correspondence,” and TODAY.com has an exclusive first look at the cover.

“The Private Correspondence,” out Dec. 1, will contain notes and letters between Alex Claremont-Diaz and Prince Henry from the day they went public with their relationship at the end of 2019’s “Red, White & Royal Blue” to 20 years later.

The epistolary novella’s cover is pink with sprayed edges and red and blue floral details. McQuiston tells TODAY.com in a statement it also contains an Easter egg; the florals on the cover are inspired by Alex and Henry’s wedding flowers.

“I could not be more in love with this cover,” McQuiston said in a statement. “We wanted to capture the handmade ephemeral vibe of a personal collection, with bits of playfully timeless Texan and English aesthetics and nods to a long and flourishing life together, all inspired by Alex and Henry’s wedding flowers.”

Fans of the “Red, White & Royal Blue” will remember that a major plot point of the book and its 2023 film adaptation is that Alex and Henry’s private emails were leaked, revealing their secret, trans-Atlantic relationship. The premise of “The Private Correspondence” establishes that “they vowed never to use email again for their personal communication,” instead relying on “handwritten love letters.”
The novella will include the characters’ personal letters, post cards and journal entries, revealing “sarcastic bon mots to sexy fantasies to emotional discussions about starting a family.”
In addition to “The Private Correspondence,” the “Red, White & Royal Blue” is getting another addition to its film universe.

In October 2025, Amazon MGM Studios revealed the title for the film’s sequel, “Red, White & Royal Wedding,” with a screenplay written by McQuiston, Gemma Burgess and Matthew Lopez. The movie entered production in January.
On their Instagram, McQuiston clarified that “The Private Correspondence” is “totally unrelated to the film sequel. It is a continuation of the book.”
They added that the novella is “spoiler-free” relative to the upcoming film sequel, adding that the film world “is slightly different from the book, and so things unfold in different ways.”

“Most of the things in (‘The Private Correspondence’) have been inside my head for years and years as the futures I envisioned for A&H,” they wrote on Instagram. “It has been so strange and surprising and joyful to come back to them a full decade after I first met them, to reconnect with the A&H who live on the page, and to have grown alongside them over the past 10 years of my own life.”

title: Romance Author Kennedy Ryan Shares the Meaningful Story Behind Her Pen Name
summary: Kennedy Ryan isn’t the author’s real name. The romance author shares the story behind her pen name and why she chooses to use it.
story: While readers may know the intimate details of romance author Kennedy Ryan’s characters, they may be less familiar with one major detail about the novelist herself: her real name.
During the June 25 episode of “Open Book with Jenna,” Ryan told Jenna Bush Hager about the meaningful backstory of her pen name, and why she had to pick one out in the first place.
After she got her first book deal, Ryan had to sit down with the HR department of the job she had at the time.
“They were like, ‘That’s great. You’re going to have to write under a pseudonym, because whatever you’re writing can’t be associated with us,” she remembers.
That human resources policy ended up benefitting her in more ways than one.
“I’m kind of glad. I like a little bit of space, I like a little distance,” she added. “I share my mental health, I share about everything.”
She’d picked out the name Kennedy Ryan long before she started writing — but it was originally for a different reason.
“I had always wanted a daughter and we had my son, and then a lot happened,” she told Jenna. “I ended up not having another child, but I always wanted to (use the) name ‘Kennedy Ryan.’”
Even as more of the world comes to know her as as bestselling author and advocate Kennedy Ryan, a few important people still stick with her real name, she told Jenna.
“Does your husband call you Kennedy?” Jenna asked.
“He’s been practicing,” Ryan replied. “Of course, at home, he’s not calling me Kennedy.”
But, she added, as she ramps up for upcoming book events, she prepares him to stick to the pseudonym.
Ryan’s latest book, “Score,” is a second chance romance and the second installment in her Hollywood Renaissance series.
With her romances, she told Jenna she wants women to realize there’s someone out there “you deserve.”
“Culture tells you the bar is way down here, and that no one’s going to clear it. Settle. I’m like, ‘Raise the bar, babe,’” she said.

title: Hallmark and Chicago Bears Will Team Up for Next ‘Holiday Touchdown’ Movie This Christmas
summary: Da Bears are ready to bring da Christmas cheer on the Hallmark Channel.
story: Da Bears are ready to bring da Christmas cheer on the Hallmark Channel.

Hallmark Media, the NFL and Skydance Sports announced in a TODAY exclusive on June 25 that they are teaming up to produce “Holiday Touchdown: A Bears Love Story,” following the success of Kansas City Chiefs and Buffalo Bills-themed Christmas movies the past two years.
The premiere date has not yet been set, but the new movie will come out later in 2026 as part of Hallmark Channel’s 17th annual Countdown to Christmas films.
Bears head coach Ben Johnson and second-year tight end Colston Loveland announced the new film in a video released on June 25.

The Bears-themed holiday movie follows “Holiday Touchdown: A Bills Love Story,” which was released in 2025. The first film of the series, “Holiday Touchdown: A Chiefs Love Story,” was released in 2024 and featured a cameo from TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager.
Both of the first two films ranked among the top cable movies of the year in total viewers, according to Nielsen.

“After the tremendous success of our first two Holiday Touchdown movies, we knew we wanted to continue the tradition by partnering with another iconic NFL franchise and fan base. The Chicago Bears have a rich history, passionate supporters, and a culture that extends far beyond the footballfield, making them a perfect fit for a heartwarming holiday story,” Hallmark Media President Darren Abbott said in a news release.

One of the NFL’s oldest and most storied franchises, the Bears have been a team on the rise under Johnson and star quarterback Caleb Williams. They won the NFC North Division title last season and beat the rival Green Bay Packers in the first round of the playoffs before falling to the Los Angeles Rams.
The new movie will be filmed entirely in and around Chicago, Hallmark said. The cast will be announced at a later date.
